data.title: HCA Healthcare Non-GAAP EPS of $6.84 beats by $0.52, revenue of $18.61B beats by $110M

data.title: Saia GAAP EPS of $2.67 beats by $0.28, revenue of $817.12M beats by $9.15M
data.description:
• Saia press release (NASDAQ:SAIA): Q2 GAAP EPS of $2.67 beats by $0.28.
  • Revenue of $817.12M (-0.7% Y/Y) beats by $9.15M.
  • Operating ratio of 87.8% compared to 83.3%.
  • LTL shipments per workday decreased 2.8%.
  • LTL tonnage per workday increased 1.1%.
  • LTL revenue per hundredweight, excluding fuel surcharge revenue, decreased 1.2%.
  • LTL revenue per shipment, excluding fuel surcharge revenue, increased 2.7%.

  • Ended the second quarter of 2025 with $18.8 million of cash on hand and total debt of $309.1 million, which compares to $11.2 million of cash on hand and total debt of $176.7 million at June 30, 2024.

  • Net capital expenditures were $375.6 million during the first six months of 2025, compared to $681.3 million in net capital expenditures during the first six months of 2024.

  • In 2025, we anticipate that net capital expenditures will be approximately $600 million to $650 million, subject to ongoing evaluation of market conditions.

data.title: AutoNation gains after earnings topper; Carvanan and CarMax also higher
data.description:
AutoNation (NYSE:AN) raced higher in early trading on Friday after the retailer topped the consensus Q2 estimates of analysts by a healthy margin.

Revenue rose 7.6% year-over-year to $7.0 billion. Same-store revenue increased 8% during the quarter from a year ago, primarily reflecting increased new vehicle unit sales and higher average new vehicle selling prices. Same-store new vehicle revenue was up 9%, while same-store used vehicle revenue increased 4%.

Same-store gross profit was up 10% from a year ago to $1.3 billion. Adjusted operating income was up 16% to $369.3 million. EPS slotted in at $5.46 vs. $4.70 consensus and $3.99 a year ago. 

On the balance sheet, AutoNation (NYSE:AN) had $1.8 billion of liquidity as of June 30, including $63 million in cash and $1.8 billion of availability under its revolving credit facility, net of commercial paper borrowings. The company's covenant leverage ratio was 2.33X at quarter end and it had $3.8 billion of non-vehicle debt outstanding. During Q2, AutoNation (AN) completed inaugural asset-backed term securitization, generating $700 million in funding for the auto loan portfolio at a weighted-average fixed interest rate of 4.90%

"We are pleased to report another quarter of strong performance, with robust growth across the entire business, including double-digit growth in Customer Financial Services and After-Sales, and improved new vehicle market share. Execution and productivity were also strong, with gross profits improving 40 basis points as a percentage of revenue," stated AutoNation (AN) CEO Mike Manley. "AutoNation's multiple revenue streams, flexible cost structure, cash flow generation, and balance sheet position us to continue delivering outstanding results and deploying capital to generate attractive shareholder returns," he added.

Shares of AutoNation (AN) were up 3.1% at 7:20 a.m. Rival CarMax (KMX) was up 0.7% and Carvana (CVNA) gained 2.7%.

data.title: Europe indexes turn red as investors read corporate results and await U.S. EU deal
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects market movements as of 7.00am ET.

London (UKX:IND) -0.39% to 9,103, UK retail sales rise lower than expected in June. Trump’s Scotland trip sparks UK push to resolve steel tariff issue, digital tax remains a sticking point. The British pound fell to $1.347, retreating further from its two-week high of $1.358. 

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.62% to 24,144. 

France (CAC:IND) +0.05% to 7,821. Consumer confidence in France edged up to 89 in July.

The unemployment rate in Hungary rose to 4.5% in the April–June.

Sweden’s unemployment rate stood at 9.4% in June. Producer prices in Sweden fell 3.1% Y/Y in June.

Total credit extended to the Greek economy increased by 6.8% from the previous year in June .

Tourist arrivals in Slovenia soared 17.2% Y/Y to 794,737 in June.

The manufacturing confidence index in Italy rose to 87.8 in July.

Bank lending to households in the Eurozone rose by 2.2% Y/Y to €7.016T in June.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.40% lower to 549.3, as investors weighed a flurry of corporate earnings, the ECB's latest policy decision, and ongoing EU-U.S. trade discussions. U.S. and EU officials are reportedly edging closer to a trade agreement that would introduce a 15% tariff on most European goods.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on charts today.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up less than 1 basis point to 4.42%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 4 basis points to 2.75%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 3 basis points to 4.65%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Three workers rescued after two-and-a-half days in collapsed Newmont mine
data.description:
Three workers who were trapped at Newmont's (NYSE:NEM) Red Chris gold and copper mine in Canada's British Columbia were rescued late Thursday after more than 60 hours underground.

The workers were safely brought to the surface following a fall-of-ground incident, resulting in the suspension of operations at the mine, which produced nearly 40K oz of gold last year.

Newmont (NYSE:NEM) said the men, who worked at the mine as contractors for drilling firm Hy-Tech Drilling, had consistent access to food, water and ventilation while stuck in an underground refuge chamber.

The company said the rescue operation involved drones and a remote-controlled scoop that dug away a massive rockfall, estimated at 65-100 ft long and 22-26 ft high.

Newmont (NEM) +1.9% pre-market Friday after reporting better than expected Q2 adjusted earnings and revenues.

data.title: First Citizens to repurchase additional up to $4B shares
data.description:
First Citizens Bancshares (NASDAQ:FCNCA) announced on Friday an additional up to $4B share repurchase plan for class A common stock.

Shares were +0.75% pre-market to $2,129.74 as the bank holding company reported second-quarter results that surpassed Wall Street expectations.

The new stock buyback program will commence upon completion of the $3.5B share repurchase plan announced in July 2024.

data.title: Gilead wins EU backing for twice-yearly HIV PrEP therapy
data.description:
Gilead (NASDAQ:GILD) announced Friday that an expert panel of the EU drug regulator, the European Medicines Agency (EMA) as part of an accelerated review, endorsed its antiviral therapy lenacapavir as a twice-yearly treatment for prevention of HIV in pre-exposure prophylaxis (PrEP).

Issuing a positive opinion, EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) has recommended the injectable for use in PrEP to cut the risk of HIV-1 infection in adults and adolescents.

The recommendation for the lenacapavir marketing authorization application will next be sent to the European Commission, which is expected to make a final decision on approval before the year-end.

Gilead (NASDAQ:GILD) expects to market lenacapavir as Yeytuo across all 27 EU member states, as well as Norway, Iceland, and Liechtenstein, subject to a favorable decision from the EC.

Last month, the company received FDA approval to market the product as Yeztugo in the U.S. as part of HIV PrEP therapy for adults and adolescents.

data.title: ChoiceOne Financial Services Non-GAAP EPS of $0.91 beats by $0.13, revenue of $42.82M beats by $1.57M
data.description:
• ChoiceOne Financial Services press release (NASDAQ:COFS): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.91 beats by $0.13.
  • Revenue of $42.82M (+90.7% Y/Y) beats by $1.57M.
  • Deposits, excluding brokered deposits, declined by $98.0 million as of June 30, 2025, compared to March 31, 2025, primarily due to seasonal municipal fluctuations and some reduction of higher cost deposits acquired from the Merger.
  • Core loans, which exclude held for sale loans and loans to other financial institutions, declined by $4.8 million or less than 1% on an annualized basis during the second quarter of 2025 and grew organically by $140.1 million or 10.0% during the twelve months ended June 30, 2025.  Core loans grew by $1.4 billion due to the Merger on March 1, 2025.  
  • The provision for credit losses on loans was $650,000 in the second quarter of 2025.

data.title: EMA issues negative opinion on Sarepta-Roche’s DMD gene therapy
data.description:
Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) (OTCQX:RHHBF) Friday said that the European Medicines Agency has issued a negative opinion on the conditional marketing authorization for Elevidys, intended for ambulatory patients aged three to seven with Duchenne muscular dystrophy.

Roche said it will continue its dialogue with the EMA to explore a potential path forward to make Elevidys available to individuals living with DMD in the EU.

In related development, Roche is pausing shipments of Elevidys in some countries, following partner Sarepta Therapeutics’ (NASDAQ:SRPT) move in the U.S., as safety concerns mount over the Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD) gene therapy.

Following three patient deaths from liver failure linked to Elevidys and another gene therapy developed by the company, Sarepta (NASDAQ:SRPT) on Monday paused U.S. shipments of the drug, approved by the FDA for the neuromuscular disorder Duchenne muscular dystrophy.

Sarepta Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SRPT) meanwhile resumed its recent sell-off on Friday after Endpoints News reported that the FDA would require the company to conduct new studies to address safety concerns and reintroduce its gene therapy, Elevidys, developed in partnership with Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY).
